title: The effects of mechanical ventilation during v-a ecmo support: a systematic review
one_line_summary: This systematic review of 12 studies on V-A ECMO patients highlights that lung-protective ventilation (lower Ppeak, DDP, RR) is associated with improved **survival**, while excessive PEEP and hyperoxia impair outcomes.
key_pearls: Implement lung-protective ventilation: Maintain low tidal volumes (4-**6 mL**/kg), keep peak inspiratory pressures (Ppeak) < 20-**22 cmH2O**, and minimize dynamic driving pressure (DDP < 14-**15 cmH2O**) to improve **survival**.

Avoid excessive PEEP: Do not routinely apply high PEEP (> **15 cmH2O**) in V-A ECMO patients. Target an individualized PEEP (around **10 cmH2O**), ideally guided by compliance or EIT, to optimize lung recruitment without compromising biventricular systolic function.

Strictly avoid hyperoxia: Avoid early systemic hyperoxia (PaO2 > **150 mmHg**) within the first 24 hours of V-A ECMO. Titrate both sweep gas FiO2 and native-lung FiO2 to keep native-lung FiO2 < 40-60%, as higher oxygen exposure is linked to increased **mortality**.

Consider iNO in ECPELLA: In patients supported with combined V-A ECMO and Impella (ECPELLA) exhibiting right ventricular dysfunction, consider initiating inhaled nitric oxide (iNO) to improve RV function and facilitate weaning.
population: Adult patients supported by veno-arterial extracorporeal membrane oxygenation (V-A ECMO).

sections.heading: Lung-Protective Ventilation Settings & Clinical Outcomes
sections.content:
Registry-Based Evidence (ELSO): Tonna et al. (2022) (n = 7,488 ECPR patients) demonstrated that lower Ppeak, lower DDP, and lower RR were independently associated with improved **survival**. Specifically, **survival** decreased with higher Ppeak > **20 cmH2O** (adjusted OR 0.69 per 1 SD increase; p < 0.001), higher DDP (adjusted OR 0.72 per 1 SD increase; p < 0.001), and higher RR (adjusted OR 0.82 per 1 SD increase; p < 0.001). Rali et al. (2023) (n = 2,226 V-A ECMO patients with cardiogenic shock) showed that survivors had **significantly** lower median Ppeak (22 vs. **24 cmH2O**; p < 0.001) and lower median DDP (14.5 vs. **16 cmH2O**; p < 0.001). Higher PEEP showed a non-**significant** trend toward improved **survival** (adjusted OR 1.08 per **1 cmH2O** increase; p = 0.14).
Clinical Bundles: Jing et al. (2022) implemented a phased, multimodal therapeutic bundle (low tidal volume [TV] of 4-**6 mL**/kg, optimal high-PEEP, and minimized FiO2) which **significantly** reduced ICU **mortality** compared to baseline practice (21.8% vs. 57.1%, p < 0.05). Gutierrez et al. (2023) observed that survivors of out-of-hospital cardiac arrest (OHCA) on V-A ECMO demonstrated progressive recovery of respiratory system compliance and consistently lower airway and driving pressures. Justus et al. (2021) noted that survivors had higher native-lung minute ventilation (7.04 vs. **5.32 L**/min, p = 0.01) and a trend toward larger TV, likely reflecting resolving lung injury and improving compliance.

sections.heading: Ventilation Modes
sections.content: Gulkarov et al. (2018) reported a small case series (n = 5) of ECPR patients transitioned to High-Frequency Percussive Ventilation (HFPV). HFPV markedly improved oxygenation, facilitated successful V-A ECMO weaning, and resulted in an 80% **survival** rate.

sections.heading: PEEP Titration & Biventricular Hemodynamics
sections.content:
EIT-Guided Titration: Soule et al. (2021) (n = 18) utilized electrical impedance tomography (EIT) combined with echocardiography. EIT-guided PEEP titration (average optimal PEEP of **10 cmH2O**) improved lung homogeneity and compliance.
Cardiac Performance: While macro-hemodynamics remained stable, echocardiography revealed that excessive PEEP (**20 cmH2O**) impaired biventricular systolic function. Right ventricular outflow tract velocity-time index (RVOT VTI) and Tricuspid Annular Plane Systolic Excursion (TAPSE) peaked at **10 cmH2O** and declined at **20 cmH2O**. Similarly, LVOT VTI was highest at **10 cmH2O** and decreased at higher pressures.
In-Silico Modeling: De Lazzari et al. (2021) used a computational cardiovascular model to show that increasing intrathoracic pressure shifted biventricular pressure-volume (P-V) loops downward and leftward. This resulted in reduced end-diastolic volume (EDV), stroke volume (SV), stroke work (SW), and pressure-volume area (PVA) in both ventricles, suggesting a cardioprotective unloading effect on the LV (reducing myocardial oxygen demand) at the cost of mild RV uncoupling.

sections.heading: Oxygenation Targets & Hyperoxia
sections.content:
Conservative vs. Liberal Targets: Burrell et al. (2024) conducted an RCT (n = 300) comparing conservative (SpO2 92-96%, PaO2 60-**90 mmHg**) vs. liberal (SpO2 97-100%, PaO2 100-**150 mmHg**) oxygenation targets. While the conservative approach successfully reduced hyperoxia, it did not **significantly** improve ICU-free days, 6-month **survival**, ECMO duration, or ICU length of stay. However, this trial was limited by a 29.5% protocol deviation rate in the conservative arm.
Hyperoxia Harm: Large registry analyses (Jentzer et al. 2023, n = 9,959; Tonna et al. 2022, n = 7,488) demonstrated that early arterial hyperoxia within 24 hours of V-A ECMO initiation was dose-dependently associated with higher in-hospital **mortality** (adjusted OR 1.14 per **50 mmHg** higher PaO2; p < 0.001). Higher native-lung FiO2 was also independently associated with **mortality**, with the highest risk observed when native-lung FiO2 exceeded 40-60%.
Preclinical Data: Edinger et al. (2022) demonstrated in a V-A ECMO rat model that anoxic ventilation (native-lung FiO2 = 0) precipitated severe cardiogenic shock, hypoxemia, acidosis, and elevated LDH, whereas native-lung FiO2 of 0.21 to 0.50 maintained stable hemodynamics.

sections.heading: Inhaled Nitric Oxide (iNO)
sections.content: Yamada et al. (2024) evaluated iNO therapy in patients supported by concurrent V-A ECMO and Impella (ECPELLA). iNO was associated with improved 30-day **survival**, higher weaning rates, shorter time to decannulation, an increased pulmonary artery pulsatility index (PAPi), and a parallel rise in Impella flow within 24 hours of initiation.

strengths_limitations:
- **Strengths**  
  - Being the **first systematic review** to comprehensively synthesize the clinical and physiological impacts of **mechanical ventilation settings** during **V‑A ECMO**, highlighting **heart‑lung interactions**.

- **Limitations**  
  - Primarily the **low quality** and **high heterogeneity** of included literature, with **ten of twelve** studies being **observational** or **preclinical**, carrying **moderate‑to‑high risks of bias**, **confounding**, and **temporal bias**.  
  - The **single RCT** also had **high protocol deviation rates**.
